texts.full_text: was a natural progression for him to start experimenting with vegan baking, given the city's diverse and health-conscious food culture. What inspired you to start making vegan doughnuts? CH: We both realized there was a significant gap in the market for high-quality vegan doughnuts. Most options available were either not that great or didn't live up to what we knew doughnuts could be. We wanted to create something that not only vegans but anyone with a sweet tooth could enjoy, without compromising on taste or texture. How do you ensure your doughnuts are both delicious and vegan? Dan: It's all about using the right ingredients and techniques. We focus on natural, high-quality ingredients like organic flours, plant-based milk, and natural sweeteners. We also pay close attention to the fermentation process, which is key to achieving that light and fluffy texture that's often missing in vegan baked goods. texts.full_text: estimate of which tank will perform best in the current patch. Brewmaster Monks are highly mobile tanks. They are unique in that they do not mitigate damage through conventional means. While other classes rely on blocks or parries, they stagger incoming damage to hit them over 10 seconds instead of having it all hit instantly. This makes them potentially the best tank at taking hard hitting attacks. This is further mitigated by activating Purifying Brew which negates 50 percent of staggered damage. As a Monk, they also supply Mystic Touch which will increase physical damage to their targets and can be used as an offensive cooldown in conjunction with other abilities for increased burst threat. However, this mobility comes at a cost; their defensive toolkit is less robust compared to some of the more traditional tanks. Brewmaster Monks rely heavily on proper management of Stagger damage over time (DoT) mechanics which requires careful attention and can be challenging during high-damage encounters or when there are multiple sources of incoming attacks. Brewmasters shine in situations where they need to move around a lot, dodge specific abilities while tanking bosses that hit hard but infrequently. They excel at maintaining threat on single targets due to their unique resource system (Chi) and the ability to weave movement with damage-dealing skills like Tiger Palm or Blackout Kick. In summary: - Strengths: High mobility; excellent for taking large, singular hits of damage. - Weaknesses: Requires careful management of Staggered Damage; less effective in high AoE situations compared to other tanks. This makes Brewmaster Monk a solid choice but not necessarily the pick depending on your raid or dungeon composition and encounter mechanics. For more traditional tanking roles with consistent, predictable patterns of damage intake, you might want to consider one of our higher-ranked options below: 5. Guardian Druid 4. Protection Warrior 3. Vengeance Demon Hunter (Havoc Spec in BfA) 2. Blood Death Knight Stay

texts.full_text: which reads a document by moving the document and scanning an image using a one-dimensional image sensor, requires that the document always moves at a constant speed in an area where the image is read. In the document reading apparatus, two rollers facing each other pinch the document therebetween, and the document is fed into the apparatus one-by-one by rotating the rollers. The document is conveyed by rotating a pair of rollers, facing each other, which is arranged in the apparatus. The document fed into the apparatus is ejected to the outside of the apparatus by the rotating rollers, starting from a front edge of the document, as reading proceeds. In case that the document is conveyed as the pair of rollers rotates, when a rear edge of the document separates from the pair of rollers, a gap, corresponding to the thickness of the document, temporarily occurs between the rollers. As shown in FIG. 8, rollers 118, 119 arranged in an apparatus pinch and convey a document 136. Therefore, at least one roller 119 is pressed by a pressi...by a pressing mechanism. This pressing mechanism ensures that the rollers maintain consistent pressure on the document, even when the rear edge temporarily separates, thereby preventing fluctuations in the document's feeding speed. The pressing mechanism, such as a spring or an adjustable pressure system, compensates for the gap by keeping the rollers in contact with the document surface. This design stabilizes the document's movement through the apparatus, ensuring uniform scanning and minimizing image distortion caused

texts.full_text: and the best conference in the profession.” Part of Jackson’s new job will be to let the public know about the caliber of education available to ISA members and to reach everyone, including the interested public, who might have the need for an appraiser. As part of its commitment to expand educational opportunities, ISA also has a new director of education in Leon Castner, ISA CAPP. Castner said that he plans to expand the line of educational programs including the development of new courses and the webinar program for delivery of short appraisal related education programs and product knowledge updates. Assisting Jackson is a growing and committed staff of professionals including Jennifer Hopkins, director of meetings, Rachel Walsh, meetings coordinator, Michelle Stearns, education and credentialing coordinator, and Sara Porter, membership coordinator. Judith Martin, ISA CAPP and president of the ISA board of directors, said “We are starting to see some real movement. We recognized a little over two years ago that our members needed to be heard and acknowledged for what they do, helping middle-America. Many of our members are generalists and they can assist families since they understand residential contents and antiques. They know how to identify items, not just from what they already know but from the ISA network of referrals through other ISA members they can depend on. Our members can fulfill their clients’ needs in times of divorce, casualty or theft loss, property division and other cases when identification and evaluation are needed.” ISA is now 30 years old and still has some of the original members. With headquarters on Chicago’s Magnificent Mile, ISA is now managed by Sentergroup, Inc, a full service association management firm. ISA proudly offers a full schedule of educational opportunities for new and veteran appraisers spread out over all of 2010 including the “Assets 2010” Conference in Toronto on April 30-May 3, 2010. texts.full_text: he applications of advanced sequencing technology enable culture-independent approaches for the detection of difficult-to-culture The applications of advanced sequencing technology enable culture-independent approaches for the detection of difficult-to-culture organisms, offering a transformative strategy for characterizing the microbial etiology of brain abscesses. Techniques such as metagenomic next-generation sequencing (mNGS) and polymerase chain reaction (PCR)-based assays have demonstrated superior sensitivity in identifying pathogens that evade traditional culture methods, including anaerobes, mycobacteria, and fastidious organisms \[[@B6-jcm-08-00351],[@B7-jcm-08-00351]\]. These methods bypass the limitations of selective media and prolonged incubation times, enabling rapid identification of both common and emerging pathogens. For instance, mNGS has been instrumental in detecting rare etiologies such as species or fungal agents in culture-negative cases \[[@B8-jcm-08-00351]\]. However, challenges persist, including the risk of contamination, the need for bioinformatics expertise to interpret complex datasets, and the potential for false positives due to environmental DNA \[[@B9-jcm-08-00351]\]. texts.full_text: na Mana' Turns 100 Years Today 'Jana Gana Mana', the country's anthem penned by Nobel Laureate Rabindranath Tagore, turned 100, as it was first sung on this day a century ago at the 1911 Calcutta Session of the National Congress. Written in highly Sanskritised Bengali in 1911 by one of the greatest poets of the 20th century, the song fired patriotism in every Indian during the IndEpendence movement. The song was adopted as the country's national anthem by the Constituent Assembly on January 24, 1950. The anthem was first performed on December 27, 1911 at the Indian National Congress session in Calcutta when the outfit was associated with the country's independence movement. Rabindranath Tagore translated "Jana Gana Mana" from Bengali to English in 1911 and also set it to music in Madanapalle, a town in Rayalaseema region of Andhra Pradesh. texts.full_text: pyrimidine dimer glycosylase excises ultraviolet radiation- and hydroxyl radical-induced products 4,6-diamino-5-formamidopyrimidine and 2,6-diamino-4-hydroxy-5-formamidopyrimidine from DNA. A DNA glycosylase specific for UV radiation-induced pyrimidine dimers has been identified from the Chlorella virus Paramecium Bursaria Chlorella virus-1. This enzyme (Chlorella virus pyrimidine dimer glycosylase [cv-pdg]) exhibits a 41% amino acid identity with endonuclease V from bacteriophage T4 (T4 pyrimidine dimer glycosylase [T4-pdg]), which is also specific for pyrimidine dimers. However, there are some differences in their substrate preferences and structural characteristics that suggest they may have evolved to adapt specifically within the unique environment of Chlorella virus or Paramecium Bursaria (PB), where DNA damage from UV radiation can be more frequent due perhaps because this location is under higher exposure risk. The enzyme cv-pdg, despite its similarity with T4 pyrimidine dimer glycosylase in sequence and function shows slight difference like the presence of a longer N‐terminal extension or hinge region which could play crucial roles during their physiological duties where specificity might be more important compared to other DNA repair pathways due for instance because these enzymes are involved mainly on lesion bypass instead most general nuclease activities. This suggests an adaptive strategy within Chlorella virus/PB strains towards optimizing specific types of damage removal that may have a greater local impact or is preferentially occurring in their host environments which makes the study significant from both evolutionary and environmental

texts.full_text: nov machine gun The Nikonov machine gun (Пулемёт Никонова (pulemot nikonova)) is a prototype double barreled light machine gun designed by Gennadiy Nikonov. Overview The weapon developed on its own initiative, out of competition and technical tasks. the weapon has no bolt, but a stationary breech and movable barrels with its own gas cylinders with piston connected to the next barrel. Upon firing one barrel, the next one was forced backwards, and thus caused the next barrel to move forward. When firing operates a feeding device that channels rounds into the barrels, with the spent cartridges ejected from both sides. The barrels move back and forth the full length of the cartridge. texts.full_text: and Z axes, may be interferometers. In In particular, interferometers can be employed to determine minute shifts in seismic wave propagation by analyzing phase changes in light waves travelling through optical fibers. These cameras usually operate by dividing a light beam into one or more paths, which are then recombined to produce an interference pattern. Seismic activity causes significant alterations in the path size or optical index of the optical medium, changing the interference pattern and producing a measurable amplitude. This principle allows high-resolution detection of acoustic and vibrational data, important for mapping subsurface geological systems. In ocean bottom seismic (OBS) systems, optical sensors can be integrated into a single optical fibre cable, providing for distributed sensing across its length. This configuration removes the need for discrete, power-hungry electronic systems at each sensor node, improving system performance in h

texts.full_text: frequency identification will really become ubiquitous in the supply chain. Vendors had been expressing concern about Wal-Mart's efforts since Rollin Ford took over as Wal-Mart's chief information officer from Linda Dillman in April. As the new CIO, Ford naturally took a cold, hard look at Wal-Mart's RFID efforts, and there was concern that he might slow down that effort or change the nature of the rollout. That would be bad news for RFID vendors, because they feel they need Wal-Mart to help create demand among other retailers who have been sitting on the sidelines watching what their rival is doing with RFID technology.

texts.full_text: up with my bf and talked to him. I told him that I think I am suffering from PMS. And it gets me mad. It wasn’t him. And then after that, whenever I am full of FURY, I take a deep breath, and I told him, I am mad. I sat down in a corner, and started crying (Yes, literally!). And for once, he didn’t run away into his cave, like he usually did. He came up to me, and hugged me and comforted me. There were times, that I was upset and out of control, and I didn’t want to believe that it was PMS. It’s kinda spooky. Cause when I go through it, it felt like as if I was being possess. I was so emotionally sure that I was right and everything else is WRONG. I was so certain about it. Yet, I need to tell myself, it’s not true. It’s just me. Just me. Discovering what I suffered from and learning how to manage it, has liberated me. And of course, protected the people I love. But it is easier said than done. Truly, mind over matter. MIND over MATTER. I have to be REALLY, rational. My advise: Ladies, do make effort to recognize it if you do suffer from it and communicate with your love ones. It really takes strength to manage it and not hurt the people around you. Men, seriously, it may be very difficult for some to handle it. Your understanding and patience matters. Seriously, I wouldn’t know what will happen to me, to us (hubby & I ), if I didn’t discover or recognize it. But somehow, thankfully, I did. It is PMS. It still visits me. I still go CRaZY at times. I have to fight it, before

texts.full_text: . Trehalose accumulation at 45 degrees C demanded an exogenous source of carbon and either glucose or glycerol fulfilled such requirement. Experiments with isogenic strains of that were either wild-type or deficient in trehalose biosynthesis (trehalose synthase mutants) revealed that trehalose accumulation was crucial for thermotolerance. The wild-type strains showed a marked increase in survival rates after a heat shock when compared to the mutants, indicating that trehalose plays a protective role during heat stress. further investigations demonstrated that trehalose acts as a stabilizer for proteins and cellular membranes, preventing denaturation and aggregation under heat stress conditions. This stabilization is thought to be one of the key mechanisms through which trehalose confers thermotolerance. On the other hand, the degradation of glycogen during heat shock suggests a metabolic shift where energy reserves are mobilized to support stress responses. glycogen breakdown provides glucose units that can be used in various metabolic pathways, including the synthesis of trehalose, thereby facilitating the rapid accumulation of this protective disaccharide. texts.full_text: - Event-Driven Model Framework October 24, 2007 · Event-driven Model Framework for ColdFusion. The name? EDM = Event-Driven Model, Mund = mouth (German), Mund = ancestral rule overthe land or domain in Old English and Germanic cultures; thus Edmund - a ruler of the event-driven model framework realm! In essence, "Edmund" signifies authority and governance within this specific software context. It streamlines development processes by utilizing an Event-Driven Model (EDM) to handle various tasks efficiently. This ColdFusion application is designed with modularity in mind for easier maintenance & scalability purposes as well ensuring better reusability across different projects when necessary without sacrificing performance dUring execution phases at runtime environments alike!

texts.full_text: "On Sunday, October 16, 2014, I worked on and mostly completed a piece titled 'Frosty Flakes' by LHN. I enjoyed the process of coloring in the black outline of the artwork, using silk HDF threads. I spent the day watching the V8 Supercars race at Bathurst on TV, which had an exciting and dramatic ending. I also watched the finale of The Block on TV that evening, which was also exciting and dramatic. I'm looking forward to starting on my next piece soon." I grabbed some time last night while dinner was cooking and added the beads before I had to go out. I'm still working on old wips. Next is anotHer small LHN ornament - Pear Tree. I'll have less stitching time for the rest of this month. I like to sit down and stitch for an hour at night when all my jobs are done, but DD has amateur theatre performances over the coming 2 weeks as well as sport and I will be out 5 nights a week with her for the next few weeks. DH is going to be in Vancouver next week

texts.full_text: more than 88 million, representing more than 20% of the population by 2050. This is why the role of a caregiver is more important than ever. People who are patient, have a passion for helping others and an interest in interacting with older adults could find caregiving to be the perfect job. Hogan outlines a few of the benefits of working in the home care industry. Flexible schedule. Caregiving requires dedication to best serve care recipients; however, caregivers can set flexible schedules that work well with other commitments such as attending school or raising children. The accommodating schedule also makes this a great job for retirees. Professional and personal growth. To be a caregiver, no prior medical experience is necessary. This career provides in-depth training opportunities to gain a variety of skills and experience. In addition, it teaches important values such as time management, organization and communication skills that can be a benefit in both personal and professional life. Opportunity to learn from an older generation and build relationships. Caring for members of the aging population offers the ability to form connections with people from different backgrounds and generations. It also allows the caregiver to develop deeper bonds with these individuals, especially during these difficult times. In the time spent together, older adults can share their decades of experience, wisdom and valuable advice with their caregivers. A rewarding experience. Caregivers work with a wide variety of clients, each with their own unique needs.
